_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to create different types of texts with well-developed, elaborated and logically sequenced ideas, using appropriate text structures and language features.

_source.w: We are learning to create different types of texts with well-developed, elaborated and logically sequenced ideas, using appropriate text structures and language features.
y: Writing with elaborated and sequenced ideas shows we are becoming skilled, independent writers.
Using appropriate text structures and features demonstrates our understanding of how texts work.
Creating a variety of text types prepares us for the diverse writing demands we will encounter.
eg: We write a narrative with well-developed characters, a detailed setting and a compelling plot.
We create an information report with logically sequenced paragraphs and supporting evidence.
We write a persuasive text with elaborated arguments and a strong conclusion.
We include appropriate language features — like technical vocabulary in an explanation or figurative language in a story.
We sequence our ideas logically, using connectives and topic sentences to guide the reader.
We create both written and spoken texts, adapting our approach for each mode.
